summary.intent: Fix 4 i18n-parity findings from Codex review pass-5 in the DateTimePicker and DatePicker components, bringing them to parity with sibling components.
summary.outcome: All 4 fixes implemented and validated: controlled→uncontrolled transition in sync_props, RTL bidi-mark filtering in DatePicker separator, localized day-period typing, and localized month aria-valuetext. All 10 CI gates green, committed, pushed, and Codex threads resolved.

repo: DateTimePicker and DatePicker have a recurring pattern of lagging behind sibling components (DateField etc.) in i18n parity — Codex multi-pass review catches these gaps systematically.
aria-valuetext for numeric segments uses unpadded values (e.g. '2' not '02'), matching DateField's aria_value_text behavior.
DatePicker's default_format_for_locale must strip bidi/control marks (e.g. RTL mark U+200F) from locale-generated separators to produce clean display separators.
Day-period typing is routed through a dedicated backend path in type_into_segment, separate from numeric segment typing.
The project uses xci-fast (10 gates) as the pre-commit CI validation step.

code.finding: sync_props needed a controlled→uncontrolled transition fix — when value prop is cleared/unset, internal state must be reset accordingly.
type_into_segment has a dedicated day-period branch that required localized character matching via day_period_from_char.
segment_attrs aria-valuetext for month/day-period segments should use aria_value_text helper to produce localized display strings.
default_format_for_locale must filter out Unicode bidi and control marks from separator characters extracted from locale format patterns.
Snapshot test for hour segment valuetext changed from padded '02' to unpadded '2' after aria_value_text fix; new test added for month-name aria-valuetext assertion.

workflow: Codex multi-pass review (pass-5 in this session) is used as an automated i18n/a11y parity auditor across component siblings.
Snapshot diffs are reviewed manually before acceptance — only accept when the change matches expected behavior (not just 'tests pass').
Spec markdown files (spec/components/date-time/*.md) are kept in sync with implementation changes as part of each fix commit.
After fixes, Codex threads are replied to and resolved, then a new Codex pass is triggered — iterative review loop.

friction: Snapshot tests changed as a side effect of the aria_value_text fix, requiring manual diff review and acceptance before the test suite was clean.
Multiple reads of the same large file (date_time_picker/mod.rs) were needed to locate different fix sites, suggesting the file is large and densely packed.
open_items: Additional Codex passes may surface further i18n-parity gaps in DateTimePicker/DatePicker — the iterative review loop continues beyond pass-5.
The controlled→uncontrolled transition pattern in sync_props may exist in other date/time components and should be audited for consistency.
